headline: Exxon expects cut of 10% to 15% in Singapore workforce by end-2027
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp expects to reduce its Singapore workforce by 10% to 15% by the end of 2027 as part of a global restructuring effort. This move will affect an estimated 500 workers out of 3,500 employees and involves relocating office staff to the Jurong plant. The company will maintain its manufacturing presence in the city-state.
label: neutral

headline: Exxon Mobil cutting 20% of its St. John's workforce amid global restructuring
summary: Exxon Mobil is set to cut 20% of its workforce in St. John's, Newfoundland, as part of a global restructuring plan affecting approximately 50 positions by the end of 2027. These layoffs will not impact offshore workers, differentiating it from broader energy sector job reductions driven by falling oil prices. This move follows a similar announcement from Imperial Oil Ltd. and is part of Exxon Mobil's strategy to leverage global technical services and technology.
label: neutral

headline: Exxon Mobil (XOM): Company Profile, Stock Price, News, Rankings
summary: This article provides a company profile for Exxon Mobil (XOM), the largest publicly traded oil and gas company in the U.S. It details the company's core businesses in exploration, production, manufacturing, and sale of crude oil, natural gas, and petrochemicals, as well as its focus on lower-emission opportunities like carbon capture and hydrogen. The piece also highlights Exxon Mobil's presence in Fortune rankings and mentions historical financial data access.
label: positive

headline: Morgan Stanley, Charles Schwab, Leidos And More On CNBC's 'Final Trades'
summary: Joe Terranova, Bryn Talkington, Jenny Van Leeuwen Harrington, and Stephen Weiss shared their "final trades" on CNBC's "Halftime Report". Terranova chose Morgan Stanley, Talkington picked iShares Ethereum Trust ETF, Harrington selected The Charles Schwab Corporation due to high share prices and Fed rate cuts, and Weiss recommended Leidos Holdings. The article also provides a brief overview of recent stock performance for these companies.
label: positive

source: ING THINK economic and financial analysis | ING THINK
headline: The Commodities Feed: Oil under pressure amid noise over more OPEC+ supply hikes
summary: Oil prices are facing downward pressure due to discussions around potential additional supply increases from OPEC+ and an expected market surplus in late 2025 and throughout 2026. Meanwhile, gold prices have surged past $3,800/oz for the first time, buoyed by a weaker USD amid US government shutdown fears, central bank purchases, and anticipated interest rate cuts. European natural gas prices also weakened despite unplanned outages, as Asian LNG demand remains soft.
label: neutral

headline: Spring-based Exxon Mobil to cut 2,000 jobs globally as part of consolidation plan
summary: Exxon Mobil (NYSE: XOM) plans to cut 2,000 jobs globally as part of a restructuring plan to consolidate smaller offices into regional hubs. These job cuts represent about 3% of Exxon's 2024 workforce and are specifically focused outside of the U.S. The company states this move aims to foster innovation, strengthen execution, and improve teamwork through enhanced collaboration.
label: neutral

headline: Exxon Mobil slashes 2000 jobs globally in restructuring move
summary: Exxon Mobil announced plans to cut 2,000 international jobs, consolidating smaller offices into regional hubs as part of a long-term restructuring. These job cuts represent approximately 3% to 4% of Exxon's global workforce and follow similar moves by other major oil companies amid falling crude prices. The restructuring aims to increase annual savings, building on the $13.5 billion already achieved under CEO Darren Woods' leadership.
label: neutral

headline: Exxon Mobil เตรียมเลิกจ้าง 2,000 ตำแหน่งทั่วโลก เดินหน้าปรับโครงสร้าง-ลดต้นทุน | การเงินธนาคาร
summary: Exxon Mobil กำลังจะเลิกจ้างพนักงานราว 2,000 ตำแหน่งทั่วโลก ซึ่งคิดเป็น 3-4% ของจำนวนพนักงานทั้งหมด เพื่อปรับโครงสร้างองค์กรและลดต้นทุน โดยจะควบรวมสำนักงานขนาดเล็กเข้ากับศูนย์ปฏิบัติการระดับภูมิภาค การปรับลดครั้งนี้เป็นส่วนหนึ่งของแผนการลดต้นทุนต่อเนื่องที่เริ่มมาตั้งแต่ปี 2562 ซึ่งช่วยประหยัดไปแล้ว 1.35 หมื่นล้านดอลลาร์ และมีเป้าหมายจะเพิ่มการประหยัดอีก 30% ภายในสิ้นทศวรรษนี้
label: positive

headline: Phoenix Energy to Begin Trading on NYSE American Under Ticker Symbol “PHXE.P”
summary: Phoenix Energy One, LLC announced that its Series A Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Shares are expected to begin trading on NYSE American under the symbol "PHXE.P". The company raised $54,080,460 million through the sale of 2,704,023 Preferred Shares at $20.00 each. A short-period initial distribution of $0.11111 per share is payable on October 15, 2025.
label: positive

headline: Exxon to Cut 2,000 Jobs
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. plans to eliminate approximately 2,000 jobs globally, representing 3% to 4% of its workforce, as part of an ongoing efficiency drive and long-term restructuring plan. These cuts, concentrated in Europe and Canada, are aimed at consolidating smaller offices into regional hubs and building on efforts to improve competitiveness and reduce annual costs, which have already seen a $13.5 billion reduction since 2019. The company's CEO, Darren Woods, indicated these changes will strengthen Exxon's market position, mirroring similar job reductions by other major oil companies.
label: neutral

headline: Exxon to Cut 2,000 Workers as Oil Sector Job Losses Mount
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp. plans to cut approximately 2,000 jobs globally, representing 3% to 4% of its workforce, as part of a long-term restructuring aimed at consolidating offices into regional hubs. These cuts, primarily in Europe and Canada, follow similar announcements by other major oil companies like Chevron, ConocoPhillips, and BP due to declining crude prices. CEO Darren Woods stated these "tough decisions" are part of an ongoing effort since 2019 to improve competitiveness and have already led to $13.5 billion in annual cost savings.
label: neutral

headline: Exxon Mobil To Slash 2,000 Jobs In Efficiency Push: Report
summary: Exxon Mobil (NYSE: XOM) plans to cut 2,000 jobs, representing 3% to 4% of its global workforce, as part of an ongoing efficiency drive and office consolidation. This follows a similar announcement from Imperial Oil Ltd., 70% owned by Exxon, to cut 20% of its workforce. These job reductions come amidst broader job losses in the oil sector due to falling crude prices.
label: neutral

headline: TechnipFMC Bags Contract for Newest Stabroek Project
summary: TechnipFMC has secured a "substantial contract" from ExxonMobil to supply subsea production systems for the Hammerhead field in Guyana's Stabroek block. This marks TechnipFMC's seventh greenfield project award from ExxonMobil Guyana, further expanding production capacity in the Stabroek block, which is targeted to reach 1.5 million bpd by 2029 with the Hammerhead project. The contract, valued between $250 million and $500 million, reinforces the ongoing development and significant investment in the region's oil and gas sector.
label: negative

headline: Reaves Utility Income Fund stock hits 52-week high at 39.08 USD
summary: The Reaves Utility Income Fund (UTG) stock has reached a 52-week high of $39.08, signifying strong investor confidence and performance over the past year. The fund boasts a 6.18% dividend yield and a 22-year history of consistent dividend payments, along with a low beta of 0.82 and a year-to-date return of 28.15%. InvestingPro offers further insights and tips for subscribers.
label: negative

headline: Venture Global Secures $1.55B for Texas Pipeline
summary: Venture Global, Inc. and WhiteWater Development LLC's joint venture, Blackfin Pipeline, has secured $1.55 billion in senior secured credit facilities. These funds will be used to develop a 3.3 Bcf/d natural gas pipeline in Texas, supporting Venture Global's operational expansion. Despite strong earnings and positive corporate events, Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates VG as Neutral due to financial instability and bearish technical indicators.
label: negative

headline: Imperial announces restructuring to further advance its well-established strategy of increasing cash flow and delivering industry-leading shareholder returns
summary: Imperial is implementing a restructuring plan to enhance its performance by centralizing corporate and technical activities in global business and technology centers, leveraging its relationship with ExxonMobil. This initiative aims to increase efficiency, advance technology deployment, and achieve $150 million in annual expense savings by 2028, all while maintaining current production and cost targets. The restructuring will involve a 20% reduction in employee roles by the end of 2027 and a one-time charge of $330 million before-tax in Q3 2025.
label: negative

headline: BP and TotalEnergies boost US energy investments
summary: BP and TotalEnergies are significantly increasing their investments in the US energy sector. BP is committing $5 billion to an offshore oil field in the Gulf of Mexico, aiming to boost its US production to over one million barrels of oil equivalent per day by 2030. Concurrently, TotalEnergies is acquiring a 49% stake in onshore gas fields in Oklahoma, further solidifying its position as a major buyer of US liquefied natural gas.
label: negative

headline: KNG ETF Holdings List — BOATS:KNG
summary: This article provides the holdings list for the KNG ETF (FT Vest S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats Target Income ETF) as of April 10, 2026. It details the top holdings including Caterpillar Inc., Target Corporation, and Linde plc, along with their respective weights, shares, and market values. The data offers insights into the fund's composition and investment strategy.
label: positive

headline: Petroleum Professional Development Center
summary: Midland College's Petroleum Professional Development Center (PPDC) is expanding scholarship opportunities for energy industry professionals thanks to a $35,000 grant from the Dawson Geophysical Scholarship Fund. The article lists a variety of upcoming courses offered at the PPDC, including safety orientations, land basics, contract marketing, geology, and technical software training, many of which are free or offer out-of-state pricing. Training topics range from basic life support and H2S clear training to advanced courses in reservoir engineering and AI.
label: positive

headline: SEC Confirms No Enforcement Recommendation for the Exxon Mobil Retail Voting Program
summary: The SEC's Division of Corporation Finance issued a no-action letter response to Exxon Mobil Corporation, confirming it would not recommend enforcement action for Exxon's proposed "Retail Voting Program." This program aims to increase retail shareholder participation in votes by allowing them to authorize standing voting instructions based on the Board's recommendations. The SEC's position was influenced by the program's limitation to retail investors, its opt-in/opt-out design, annual reminders, and participants' ability to override instructions for upcoming meetings.
label: positive

headline: Fuel Additives Market Size, Share | CAGR of 4.3%
summary: The global Fuel Additives Market is projected to reach USD 12.2 billion by 2034, growing from USD 8.0 billion in 2024 at a CAGR of 4.3%. Key drivers include the increasing demand for cleaner and more efficient fuels, stricter emission regulations, and the expanding role of additives in biofuels. Deposit control additives and diesel applications currently dominate the market segments, with North America holding the largest regional share.
label: negative

headline: BP Retracts View Oil Demand Could Peak 2025
summary: BP has retracted its previous projection that oil demand could peak as early as 2025, now stating that demand will continue to grow until at least 2030, possibly reaching 103.4 million barrels a day in five years. This shift is attributed to rising consumption in emerging markets, sluggish energy efficiency gains, geopolitical tensions, and the persistent use of petrochemicals. The company also highlights increasing natural gas demand, especially in Asia, and the growing electricity consumption from data centers due to AI.
label: neutral

headline: Eni, Exxon Among Companies Fined in Italy for Fuel Price Fixing
summary: Eni SpA and Exxon Mobil Corp.'s Esso unit, along with other refiners and fuel distributors in Italy, have been fined approximately $1 billion for allegedly fixing fuel prices. Italy's competition watchdog claims the companies colluded to set the value of a biofuel component. Several of the accused companies have denied the allegations.
label: neutral

headline: Energy Outlook | Energy economics | Home
summary: BP's Energy Outlook 2025 explores the future of the energy transition to 2050 through two scenarios: "Current Trajectory" and "Below 2°". The report examines emerging trends in energy demand, security, efficiency, and carbon emissions, highlighting the growing importance of renewables and the declining role of fossil fuels. It also considers sensitivities such as geopolitical fragmentation and weak energy efficiency gains.
label: positive

headline: SLB picks up job with Petrobras for up to 35 wells in Brazilian waters
summary: SLB has secured a major contract with Petrobras to provide services and technology for up to 35 ultra-deepwater wells in the Atapu and Sépia fields in the Santos Basin, Brazil. This assignment involves deploying advanced electric completions technologies and digital solutions to enhance oil and gas production, with work scheduled to begin in mid-2026. This follows another significant contract awarded to the SLB OneSubsea joint venture for these same fields in 2024.
label: negative
